prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: TaraHome blog cover, landscape 3:2 / 1536x1024 crop-safe
Primary request: Create a unique topic-specific cover image for an article titled "How to Set Up a Home Assistant Wall Tablet Dashboard That Stays Awake" about a reliable Home Assistant wall tablet kiosk dashboard.
Scene/backdrop: warm residential kitchen or hallway wall with a real-looking wall-mounted tablet showing a blurred, unreadable smart-home dashboard layout, plus a small local smart-home hub or compact mini PC on a nearby shelf. The scene should feel like a practical homeowner's local automation setup, not a showroom or data center.
Subject: the wall tablet is the main subject; include subtle dark-green local-control accent lighting, a nearby presence/motion sensor, and a tidy power cable or discreet mount where relevant.
Style/medium: clean photographic/editorial smart-home interior, premium practical technology photography, realistic materials, no illustration, no diagram.
Composition/framing: landscape cover, subject useful at small card size, tablet slightly off-center with clean negative space around edges for cropping; no inset frames.
Lighting/mood: warm natural window light, calm, trustworthy, lived-in but tidy.
Color palette: warm residential neutrals, off-white walls, wood or stone textures, dark green local-control accents, muted gray/blue hardware; avoid purple gradients.
Text: no text.
Constraints: no embedded headline, no title poster, no readable UI text, no logos, no brand names, no charts, no graphs, no diagrams, no floating UI cards, no translucent panels, no inset frames, no progress bars, no decorative routing/network/path line overlays, no people, no hands, no messy cable tangle, no generic AI network graphics, no fake chart filler.
